cases.name: Dutch Female Passenger (Index)

flights.notes: Seat unconfirmed — boarded SHN symptomatic, deteriorated in-flight

cases.clinical_notes: Index case. Dutch female passenger (age 69) aboard MV Hondius. Before boarding in Ushuaia on April 1, she and her husband traveled in Argentina, Chile, and Uruguay (Al Jazeera / WHO, May 7 2026). Argentine authorities' leading hypothesis (per AP, May 7 2026): couple contracted hantavirus during a bird-watching outing in Ushuaia; investigators also tracing their route through Patagonian forested hillsides. Province of Tierra del Fuego — where ship docked — had never previously recorded a hantavirus case. Disembarked at Saint Helena on April 24 with gastrointestinal symptoms. Boarded Airlink flight 4Z 301 (SHN→JNB) on April 25 — condition deteriorated in-flight. Died April 26 at Johannesburg hospital; tested positive for Andes-strain hantavirus. WHO initiated contact tracing for all 82 passengers and 6 crew on that flight. KLM confirmed she also traveled on a KLM flight from Johannesburg. Husband (P002) died on the ship April 11. [Note: P34866 in database is likely a duplicate of this case — same person described as '69-year-old woman who disembarked at St Helena and died in South Africa'.]

cases.name: Dutch Male Passenger (Husband of Index)

location.venue: MV Hondius — underway between South Georgia and Tristan da Cunha

cases.source_notes: Manually entered — search ProMED or WHO DON for 'Hondius hantavirus 2026'
cases.clinical_notes: Dutch male passenger (age 70), husband of index case PEDB43, sharing Cabin 406. Per WHO/USA Today May 7 official summary: developed fever, headache, and mild diarrhea on April 6; died aboard MV Hondius on April 11 — the first death in the outbreak (5 days after symptom onset). Important: no samples were taken at time of death and hantavirus was not initially identified. Argentine authorities' leading hypothesis (AP, May 7 2026): couple contracted hantavirus during a bird-watching outing in Ushuaia before boarding on April 1. WHO confirmed couple traveled Argentina, Chile, AND Uruguay prior to boarding (Al Jazeera, May 7 2026). Investigators also tracing their route through forested hillsides of Patagonia. Tierra del Fuego province had never previously recorded a hantavirus case. Body removed to St. Helena on April 24. Early reporting erroneously described him as evacuated to Punta Arenas — corrected to 'died on the ship' per WHO/AFP/Manila Times.

cases.name: British Guest — Symptomatic Evacuee (April 27)
cases.source_notes: Manually entered — search MRCC Cape Town / SA health authorities for April 2026 evacuation records
cases.clinical_notes: British male GUEST (not crew — corrected per Oceanwide Expeditions official timeline, USA Today May 8 2026). Reported fever, shortness of breath, and signs of pneumonia to the ship's doctor on April 24. Condition worsened; medically evacuated April 27 via Ascension Island alongside his American partner (see PUSPAR01) — both removed from the ship together as the total was reduced from 149 to 147. Tested positive for hantavirus on May 2 in South Africa — making him the FIRST laboratory-confirmed case in the outbreak (AP, May 7 2026). UPDATE May 13: South African health ministry spokesperson told Reuters patient is 'improving, gradually so' but 'still ill.' Previously reported as 'doing better' by WHO May 8.
cases.reporter: MRCC Cape Town / SA Medical Services

cases.name: Dutch Crew Member

location.venue: MV Hondius — Crew Deck 2 / Engine Room

cases.source_notes: Serology pending as of May 5 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Crew member sharing workspace and common ventilation zones with P003 during P003's prodromal phase. Fever, myalgia, early respiratory symptoms. Serology pending. Vessel stranded near Cape Verde — under shipboard medical monitoring awaiting evacuation. [Note: PDB8D5 is also Dutch age 41 — possible same person; retain both pending confirmation.]

cases.name: Cape Verde Evacuee — Mild Symptoms (possible duplicate)
cases.clinical_notes: ⚠️ POSSIBLE DUPLICATE — May be same person as PA1B22, P872E8, or PDB8D5 (the three officially listed May 6 evacuees to the Netherlands per WHO/Oceanwide). Auto-scraped entry for ill crew member with mild symptoms requiring urgent medical evacuation from the stranded cruise ship near Cape Verde. Retain pending confirmation of distinct identity.

cases.name: Cape Verde Evacuee — Severe Symptoms (possible duplicate)
cases.clinical_notes: ⚠️ POSSIBLE DUPLICATE — May be same person as PA1B22, P872E8, or PDB8D5 (the three officially listed May 6 evacuees to the Netherlands per WHO/Oceanwide). Auto-scraped entry for ill crew member with severe symptoms requiring urgent medical evacuation from the stranded cruise ship near Cape Verde. Retain pending confirmation of distinct identity.

cases.name: MV Hondius Ship's Doctor (Evacuated, Serious)
cases.source_notes: Oceanwide Expeditions statement / NBC News May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Ship's doctor aboard MV Hondius. Identity and role confirmed by Oceanwide Expeditions (ship operator) via NBC News (May 8 2026). Evacuated May 6 from ship near Cape Verde — one of three people evacuated that day — taken to specialized hospital in Europe for treatment. Was in serious condition at time of evacuation. Oceanwide Expeditions confirmed the ship's doctor is among those being treated.

cases.name: Swiss MV Hondius Passenger
cases.clinical_notes: Swiss man aboard MV Hondius, disembarked April 24-25 and took the April 25 Airlink flight from St Helena to Johannesburg (~80 passengers). Returned from South Africa with his wife in late April. Now confirmed positive and receiving treatment at hospital in Zurich. Per El País, he is the confirmed positive case on the South Africa flight that prompted France to trace 8 French citizens also aboard.

cases.name: Suspected Case — Fever Resolved
cases.clinical_notes: Passenger aboard MV Hondius who developed fever (key hantavirus symptom) which subsequently resolved; retained as suspected case pending PCR confirmation.

cases.name: Dutch Passenger, 41, Hondius
cases.clinical_notes: 41-year-old Dutch national evacuated from MV Hondius and flown to Amsterdam for specialized hospital treatment. [Note: P004 is also Dutch age 41, labeled crew with Apr 18 onset — possible same person; retain both pending confirmation.]

cases.name: German Passenger (Deceased, May 2)
cases.clinical_notes: Third death in the MV Hondius outbreak. Official WHO/Oceanwide listing (USA Today, May 8 2026) confirms symptom onset April 28 — fever and signs of pneumonia. Died May 2, 2026 — 4 days after symptom onset. Earlier Al Jazeera/WHO May 7 report of April 25 onset appears to have been a reporting error; official source is April 28. Nationality reported as German. Confirmed by WHO as third fatality.

cases.name: German Passenger, 65, Hondius (suspected)
cases.clinical_notes: 65-year-old German evacuated as suspected case; asymptomatic but closely associated with a German passenger who died on May 2.

cases.name: British Man Evacuee, MV Hondius
cases.clinical_notes: British man evacuated from MV Hondius cruise ship to the Netherlands after displaying hantavirus symptoms.

cases.name: May 6 Evacuee — Possible Duplicate
cases.clinical_notes: ⚠️ POSSIBLE DUPLICATE — Auto-scraped. WHO/Oceanwide official listing accounts for exactly 3 May 6 evacuees to the Netherlands, already tracked as PA1B22 (ship's doctor), P872E8 (British man), and PDB8D5 (Dutch 41yo). This entry may be redundant. Retain pending confirmation.

cases.name: Cruise Ship Passenger Case 1
cases.clinical_notes: Confirmed case of Andes strain hantavirus linked to cruise ship outbreak.

cases.name: May 7 British Evacuee — Possible Duplicate
cases.clinical_notes: ⚠️ POSSIBLE DUPLICATE — Auto-scraped. Not included in official WHO/Oceanwide listing of 8 cases. May overlap with one of the 3 confirmed May 6 Netherlands evacuees. Retain pending confirmation of distinct identity.

cases.name: Airlink Flight Attendant (Suspected)
cases.source_notes: USA Today May 7 / dutchnews.nl / Dutch Health Ministry
cases.clinical_notes: Dutch flight attendant on Airlink flight 4Z 301 (SHN→JNB, April 25). Index case PEDB43 was on this flight but was too ill to take her onward international flight to Europe and was taken off the plane in Johannesburg, where she died. The Airlink attendant is now showing hantavirus symptoms and being tested in an isolation ward at Amsterdam hospital (Dutch Health Ministry via AP, May 7 2026). If confirmed positive, would be the FIRST known person not aboard MV Hondius to be infected — representing Gen 2 transmission (ship passenger → airline crew). 88 people total were on the April 25 SHN→JNB flight. IMPORTANT DISTINCTION (NBC News, May 8 2026): A separate KLM flight attendant who was NOT on flight 4Z 301 tested NEGATIVE for hantavirus — this does NOT apply to PKLM01 (the Airlink attendant). The KLM negative result is a different person on a different flight.
cases.reporter: Dutch Health Ministry / USA Today / dutchnews.nl / NBC News

cases.name: Singapore Men — Isolated & Tested (2)
cases.source_notes: AP, May 7 2026 — Singapore Ministry of Health
cases.clinical_notes: Two Singaporean men who disembarked MV Hondius at Saint Helena on April 24, flew to South Africa, then returned home to Singapore at different times. Singapore health authorities confirmed both are being isolated and tested as of May 7. Part of the 30–40 untraced St Helena departures (PGRP01).

cases.name: French Citizen — Contact Case, Benign Symptoms
cases.source_notes: AP, May 7 2026 — French Health Ministry statement
cases.clinical_notes: One of 8 French citizens identified by French authorities as having been on the April 25 Airlink flight (St Helena → Johannesburg, ~80 passengers) with confirmed Swiss case P3BE87. This individual has benign symptoms and is in isolation undergoing medical tests. The other 7 French citizens are being monitored as asymptomatic contacts.

cases.name: Virginia Resident — Under Monitoring
cases.source_notes: USA Today live updates, May 7 2026 — Virginia Dept of Health spokesperson Maria Reppas
cases.clinical_notes: One Virginia resident returned from MV Hondius, in good health, under public health monitoring as of May 7. Virginia Dept of Health says 'less than five exposed Virginians' may be identified in coming days. Virginia is the 5th US state to report returned passengers alongside California, Arizona, Texas, and Georgia. Risk to general public considered low.
cases.reporter: Virginia Department of Health / USA Today

cases.name: Texas Residents — Self-Monitoring (2)
cases.source_notes: USA Today live updates, May 7 2026 — Texas Dept of Health
cases.clinical_notes: Two Texas residents who were MV Hondius passengers returned to the state before the outbreak was identified. No symptoms reported. Left ship before contact tracing began; believed not to have had direct contact with an infected person. Agreed to self-monitor in coming days. Texas is among 5 US states (CA, AZ, TX, GA, VA) monitoring returned ship passengers.
cases.reporter: Texas Department of Health / USA Today

cases.name: Coastal Georgia Woman — MV Hondius Passenger
cases.source_notes: WTOC News, May 8 2026 — Coastal Georgia local TV report
cases.clinical_notes: Woman from coastal Georgia who was a passenger aboard MV Hondius. Returned home and is among residents being monitored by Georgia health authorities. Georgia is one of 5 US states (CA, AZ, TX, GA, VA) tracking returned ship passengers. No confirmed illness reported.
cases.reporter: WTOC / Georgia Dept of Public Health

cases.name: Bariloche Male Patient (Argentina Domestic, 2026 — NOT MV Hondius)
cases.source_notes: AP / Isabel Debre, May 7 2026 — Río Negro Province government announcement
cases.clinical_notes: Male patient. First hantavirus case of 2026 recorded in Bariloche, Patagonia's most popular mountain resort town and northern entry point. Río Negro Province government announced the case on Tuesday May 6; patient was hospitalised on Wednesday May 6. Andes virus (rodent-borne). Part of Argentina's elevated 2025–26 season — 101 hantavirus infections nationally since June 2025, roughly double the prior-year caseload. UNRELATED to MV Hondius cruise ship cluster.

cases.name: Saint Helena Departures — Untraced (30–40 passengers)
cases.source_notes: AP / KCRA May 7 2026
cases.clinical_notes:
VOYAGE TIMELINE (Oceanwide Expeditions official, via USA Today May 8 2026):
Apr 1: Departed Ushuaia — 175 aboard (114 guests, 61 crew).
Apr 4-7: Stop at South Georgia Island.
Apr 13-16: Stop at Tristan da Cunha/Inaccessible/Nightingale Islands — 6 guests embark (islanders), 1 crew disembarks → 180 total (120 guests, 60 crew).
Apr 17: Stop at Gough Island.
Apr 21-24: Stop at St. Helena — 1 crew embarks, 32 guests disembark (including Dutch couple P002/PEDB43) → 149 total (88 guests, 61 crew).
Apr 27: 2 guests medevaced via Ascension Island — British national P003 (symptomatic) and American partner PUSPAR01 (precautionary) → 147 total (86 guests, 61 crew).

CURRENT SHIP MANIFEST (Oceanwide, USA Today May 8) by nationality: Argentina 1g, Australia 4g, Belgium 2g, Canada 4g, France 5g, Germany 5g+1c, Greece 1g, Guatemala 1c, India 2c, Ireland 2g, Italy 1g, Japan 1g, Montenegro 1c, Netherlands 8g+5c, New Zealand 1g, Philippines 38c, Poland 1c, Portugal 1c, Russia 1c, Spain 13g+1c, Turkey 3g, UK 19g+3c, Ukraine 5c, USA 17g. (g=guest, c=crew)

Countries notified by WHO (May 7): Canada, Denmark, Germany, Netherlands, New Zealand, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Singapore, Sweden, Switzerland, Turkey, UK, USA. Also South Africa and Spain.

St. Helena authorities monitoring a 'small number' of higher-risk contacts ordered to isolate 45 days. Singapore monitoring 2 men (PUSSG01). 7 Americans across 6 US states (AZ, CA, GA, NJ, TX, VA) monitored — none symptomatic. New Jersey monitoring 2 (PUSNJ01). US biocontainment charter to Nebraska planned for 17 Americans. Ship docked Granadilla port, Tenerife, Sunday May 10. All passengers disembarked and repatriated to six European countries, Canada, and USA by May 11. MV Hondius departed Tenerife May 12, returning to Netherlands (ETA May 17-18 per ECDC May 13 update). Crew remain aboard for return voyage.
cases.reporter: AP / KCRA / CDPH / CNN / WHO / NBC News / Calgary CityNews

cases.name: Alicante Woman — Flight Contact (Gen 2)

location.venue: Hospital — Alicante (admitted; samples sent to National Microbiology Center)

cases.source_notes: Spain Health Minister Mónica García via X / LiveScience, May 8 2026; NBC News May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: 32-year-old Spanish woman hospitalised in Alicante (NOT home-isolating) after travelling on a flight 'close to' the Dutch woman (PEDB43) who died of hantavirus. Seated two rows behind PEDB43 on the flight. Reporting mild symptoms compatible with hantavirus. Admitted to Sant Joan Hospital in negative pressure isolation room. Gen 2 case — flight contact with index case, not aboard MV Hondius. Same transmission pathway as PFRCE01 (French citizen) and PKLM01 (Airlink flight attendant). UPDATE May 12-13: TESTED NEGATIVE TWICE by Spain's National Microbiology Centre. Remains hospitalised pending mandatory third PCR test per protocol. Condition stable with only mild respiratory symptoms (cough). Likely to be ruled out. Spain health minister: 'risk for the general population remains very low.'
cases.reporter: Spain Health Minister Mónica García / LiveScience / NBC News / Alicante Today

cases.name: British Male — Tristan da Cunha Disembarkation

location.country: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha

cases.source_notes: UK Health Security Agency statement, May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: British male passenger — Tristan da Cunha islander/resident. The ship stopped at Tristan da Cunha (including Inaccessible Island and Nightingale Island) April 13-16. Per Oceanwide official timeline: 6 guests embarked at Tristan da Cunha (islanders hitching a ride), and 1 crew member disembarked. PTRISTAN01 is one of those 6 who boarded at Tristan. He is currently hospitalised on the island after developing symptoms. Spouse is isolating as a precaution. Tristan da Cunha has a population of just over 200 people and is one of the most remote permanently inhabited islands on Earth. Identified as a new suspected case by UK Health Security Agency on May 8, 2026. Additionally, 4 other Tristan islanders who had been off-island hitched a ride back home on MV Hondius to St Helena, then onward — their status is being monitored.
cases.reporter: UK Health Security Agency / LiveScience / NBC News

cases.name: Arizona Resident — MV Hondius Passenger
cases.source_notes: CNN / Arizona Department of Health, May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Arizona resident who was a passenger on MV Hondius. Has not had symptoms and is being monitored by Arizona Department of Health public health workers. Arizona is among the 5 US states (CA, AZ, TX, GA, VA) monitoring returned ship passengers. US sending plane to Canary Islands to repatriate 17 Americans from the ship.

cases.name: Spanish Passengers Group (14) — Military Hospital
cases.source_notes: CNN / Spain Health Minister Mónica García, May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: 14 Spanish passengers aboard MV Hondius transported to Gómez Ulla Military Hospital in Madrid after disembarkation at Tenerife (May 10). Full protocol per Spain head of emergency services Virginia Barcones: PCR test on arrival + repeat PCR on day 7; if positive → UATAN unit; single rooms, no visitor contact. UPDATE May 12: ONE CONFIRMED POSITIVE — one Spanish passenger tested positive (initially provisional on May 11, confirmed May 12 by second PCR). Patient was initially asymptomatic but later developed low-grade fever and mild respiratory symptoms. Currently stable on floor 22 with maximum isolation, no clinical deterioration. The OTHER 13 passengers ALL TESTED NEGATIVE on first PCR. Confirmed case is now tracked separately as PESP_GU01. Spain health ministry set May 10 as 'day zero' for quarantine (up to 42 days).
cases.reporter: CNN / Spain Health Ministry / Al Jazeera / Euronews / The Spanish Eye

cases.name: New Jersey Residents — Post-Disembarkation Exposure (2)
cases.source_notes: CNN, May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Two New Jersey residents potentially exposed to a person infected with hantavirus after that person departed MV Hondius. NOT aboard the ship — represents post-disembarkation contact exposure. Not experiencing hantavirus symptoms as of May 8. New Jersey is the 6th US state (alongside AZ, CA, GA, TX, VA) to report cases under monitoring. Generation listed as 2 (contact with a ship passenger, not the ship itself).
cases.reporter: CNN / New Jersey Department of Health

cases.name: Alberta Residents — Flight Contacts (2), Isolating at Home 21+ days
cases.source_notes: The Canadian Press (Nicole Ireland / Hannah Alberga), May 13 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Two Alberta residents possibly exposed to hantavirus on a flight — NOT aboard the ship. Isolating at home in Alberta for at least 21 days from time of possible exposure. No symptoms as of May 13. Part of 10 total people in Canada with connections to the outbreak: 6 ship passengers + 4 flight contacts. Gen 2 — post-disembarkation flight contact. Testing only begins if symptoms develop; experts warn initial Andes virus symptoms are 'tricky' — easily mistaken for cold, headache, or stress. David Safronetz (National Microbiology Lab, Winnipeg): 'The question is always going to be, is the person truly negative because they were never actually infected, or are they negative because at the time that sample was collected, the virus just isn't present at high enough levels?'
cases.reporter: The Canadian Press / Alberta Ministry of Health / National Microbiology Laboratory

cases.name: Ontario/Peel Region Residents — Flight Contacts (8 total: 1 isolating + 7 low-risk)

location.venue: Isolating — Peel region (1 primary) + 7 secondary contacts isolating as of May 13

cases.source_notes: The Canadian Press, May 13 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Primary: 1 visitor to Canada (not a ship passenger) who was on a flight with a ship passenger who later died of hantavirus — isolating in Peel region, Ontario. On Tuesday May 13, Ontario's Ministry of Health asked 7 additional people to isolate as 'low risk' contacts — they had come into contact with this higher-risk Peel region person. Total Ontario flight contact network: 8 people. No symptoms as of May 13. Gen 2 case. David Safronetz (National Microbiology Lab): testing not reliable pre-symptoms given long incubation. All tests sent to provincial labs then National Microbiology Laboratory in Winnipeg for confirmation.
cases.reporter: The Canadian Press / Ontario Ministry of Health

cases.name: Canadian Ship Passengers — Back in Canada (6 total, 4 locations)

location.city: Vancouver Island / Grey Bruce / Ontario / Quebec

cases.source_notes: The Canadian Press (Nicole Ireland / Hannah Alberga), May 13 2026
cases.clinical_notes:
UPDATE May 13 2026 — 6 ship passengers back in Canada across 4 locations:
(1) Vancouver Island, BC — 4 passengers: a couple in their 70s from the Yukon; a person in their 70s from Vancouver Island; a person from BC in their 50s who lives abroad. 21-day quarantine started Sunday May 11 — could extend to 42 days. Dr. Bonnie Henry (BC PHO) confirmed.
(2) Grey Bruce region, Ontario — 2 passengers (a couple), isolating 45 days.
(3) One Quebecer possibly exposed on a flight — was isolating until Monday May 11, then deemed low risk; now self-monitoring 42 days.
Total Canada: 10 people (6 ship passengers + 4 flight contacts). None symptomatic as of May 13.

UPDATE May 16 2026 — Yukon passenger (part of Vancouver Island group) returned PRESUMPTIVE POSITIVE for Andes hantavirus. See PCAN04 for individual case. Their spouse has mild symptoms and was also hospitalized. A third person was taken to hospital for assessment out of abundance of caution. Dr. Bonnie Henry: quarantine timeline may now be extended to 42 days for all VI passengers. Canada total monitoring: 36 people (per PHAC May 16), including 4 ship passengers currently in isolation. All 4 Vancouver Island passengers confirmed to have had no contact with the public since arrival. NML Winnipeg confirmatory results expected within 2 days.
cases.reporter: The Canadian Press / Dr. Bonnie Henry (BC PHO) / PHAC

cases.name: Canadian Passenger — Yukon, CONFIRMED POSITIVE · 10th case from MV Hondius
cases.source_notes: NBC News / BBC / Public Health Agency of Canada, May 16 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Yukon resident, couple in their 70s — one of four Vancouver Island group passengers isolating in BC. Developed symptoms and was hospitalized Thursday May 15 along with spouse. Returned PRESUMPTIVE POSITIVE on Friday May 16 (BC PHO Dr. Bonnie Henry). UPDATE May 18: PHAC OFFICIALLY CONFIRMED positive for Andes hantavirus — the 10th confirmed case from MV Hondius. Spouse tested NEGATIVE. Both remain in hospital in Victoria, BC in isolation. Dr. Bonnie Henry: 'Clearly, this is not what we hoped for, but it is what we planned for.' A third person isolating in secure lodging also taken to hospital for assessment 'out of abundance of caution' — connection to couple unclear. All four Vancouver Island passengers confirmed to have had no contact with the general public since arriving in Canada. PHAC: 'All confirmed cases to date have been passengers or crew on the MV Hondius cruise ship. The overall risk to the general population in Canada remains low.' This is the first confirmed case among passengers who had returned home after disembarkation.
cases.reporter: NBC News / BBC / PHAC / ABC7 / Dr. Bonnie Henry

cases.name: American Passenger — Partner of British Evacuee (P003)

location.venue: Evacuated via Ascension Island — current location unknown

cases.source_notes: Oceanwide Expeditions official timeline via USA Today, May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: American passenger aboard MV Hondius — partner of British national P003. Per Oceanwide Expeditions official timeline: on April 27 "two guests are medevaced via Ascension Island: a symptomatic British national [P003] and the patient's American partner." This reduces ship total from 149 to 147. Evacuation appears precautionary for the American partner (P003 described as the symptomatic one). Current location and health status unknown — may be in South Africa with P003, or may have returned to the US (possibly one of the 7 Americans under state monitoring). NEW CASE — not previously in database. Not to be confused with the 17 Americans still aboard the ship.

cases.name: California Residents (4) — MV Hondius Exposure
cases.source_notes: California Department of Public Health news release, May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Four California residents exposed to Andes hantavirus — updated May 13 2026 (up from 1 reported May 8). 2 are quarantined; 2 are monitored by local health departments (California Dept of Public Health). All asymptomatic as of May 13. Protocol: daily temperature checks, assessment for hantavirus-consistent symptoms. California is one of 10 US states monitoring returned passengers/contacts as of May 13.
cases.reporter: California Department of Public Health / USA Today

cases.name: French Contacts — 26 in hospital isolation, ALL NEGATIVE (May 14)

location.venue: Hospital isolation — all tested negative as of May 14

cases.source_notes: Arab News / Reuters / JPost — French Health Minister Rist, May 14 2026
cases.clinical_notes: UPDATE May 14 2026: ALL 26 TESTED NEGATIVE. French Health Minister Stephanie Rist confirmed on X/Twitter that all 26 people in hospital isolation in France have tested negative for hantavirus — 'without exception.' They will continue to be monitored and tested 3 times per week. Health authorities will no longer communicate results unless there is a positive test. Breakdown of the 26: (1) 22 flight contacts — passengers on the April 25 Airlink flight SHN→JNB and/or the Johannesburg→Amsterdam KLM flight on which PEDB43 (Dutch index case) was traveling when she deteriorated. She was removed from the JNB→AMS flight and died in South Africa. (2) 4 French ship passengers currently in isolation at Bichat Hospital. A 5th French ship passenger (PFRCE03) is confirmed positive and critically ill on ECMO — not included in this negative count. French count grew from 7 (May 9) to 22 (May 13) to 26 total (May 14) as contact tracing expanded.
cases.reporter: Arab News / Reuters / JPost / French Health Minister Rist

cases.name: KLM Flight Attendant — Amsterdam UMC (NEGATIVE — Ruled Out)

exposures.venue: Johannesburg O.R. Tambo International Airport — aircraft cabin
exposures.notes: Brief cabin interaction with a passenger (duration: minutes). Passenger connection to MV Hondius under investigation.

cases.source_notes: Reuters / WHO, May 8 2026
cases.clinical_notes: ⚠️ RULED OUT — TESTED NEGATIVE. KLM Royal Dutch Airlines flight attendant admitted to Amsterdam UMC on May 8 with suspected hantavirus after brief cabin contact with a passenger at Johannesburg O.R. Tambo Airport. WHO confirmed negative test result on May 8 (Reuters). Was NOT aboard MV Hondius. Contact lasted 'a matter of minutes' per KLM. Kept in database as an epidemiologically significant ruled-out case: demonstrates brief aircraft cabin contact did NOT result in transmission in this instance. Provides reassurance on transmission risk from incidental passenger contact.

cases.name: Barcelona Passenger — Hospital Clínic (Asymptomatic)
cases.source_notes: El País, May 9 2026
cases.clinical_notes: MV Hondius passenger admitted to Hospital Clínic de Barcelona, asymptomatic. Traveling to Barcelona after disembarkation. Identified via El País contact tracing graphic. No symptoms reported.

cases.name: Tristan da Cunha Resident — Suspected Hantavirus (Stable)

location.venue: Tristan da Cunha — remote island, no airstrip

cases.source_notes: Reuters / UK MoD, May 10 2026
cases.clinical_notes: British man — MV Hondius passenger. Ship docked at Tristan da Cunha April 13–15; patient is one of 6 islanders who boarded. Reported symptoms April 28 per WHO. Stable and in isolation. Oxygen supplies on island at critical level. UK response May 10: 6 paratroopers + 2 military clinicians from 16 Air Assault Brigade parachuted from RAF A400M — flew 6,788 km RAF Brize Norton (Oxfordshire) → Ascension Island, then 3,000 km south to Tristan da Cunha; mid-air refuelled by RAF Voyager tanker. Oxygen + medical equipment airdropped. UK MoD: first time British military has deployed medical personnel via parachute for humanitarian support. Tristan da Cunha pop. ~200 — world's most remote inhabited island; normally 6-day boat from St Helena, nearest inhabited neighbour. Usual medical provision: 2-person team. Separate note: PCR tests delivered to Ascension Island by military plane May 7 for another British man (P003) who disembarked there and was medevaced to South Africa.

cases.name: French Repatriated Passenger — PCR Confirmed, CRITICALLY ILL (Bichat Hospital, Paris)

location.venue: Bichat Hospital, Paris — ICU, artificial lung (ECMO)

flights.notes: French government repatriation flight — Dassault Falcon 900EX (F-HREG), call sign ARL120E. TFS → Paris Le Bourget (LBG). Was symptomatic during flight.

cases.source_notes: ABC News live updates / French Health Minister Stéphanie Rist, May 11 2026
cases.clinical_notes: French MV Hondius passenger — PCR confirmed positive. Was asymptomatic when leaving the ship (per ECDC briefing, Stockholm, May 13). Became critically ill after repatriation. As of May 13 2026: on ECMO (extracorporeal membrane oxygenation) — artificial lung — at Bichat Hospital, Paris. Dr. Xavier Lescure (infectious disease, Bichat): device pumps blood through artificial lung providing oxygen and returning it to the body — 'the final stage of supportive care.' French Health Minister Rist (May 13): condition deteriorating. Hope is that ECMO relieves pressure on lungs/heart to allow recovery. One of three passengers globally critically ill (ECDC). 22 French contact cases identified. Repatriated ARL120E TFS→LBG May 10.
cases.reporter: NYT / French Health Ministry / ECDC / USA Today

cases.name: Dr. Stephen Kornfeld — Retired Oncologist, Bend OR — UNMC Quarantine Unit (Subsequent Tests NEGATIVE)

location.venue: National Quarantine Unit — UNMC (moved from biocontainment May 15)

transport.notes: US DoS airlift — TFS → Washington D.C. → Nebraska (UNMC)

cases.source_notes: HHS statement / Nebraska Medicine / ABC News, May 11 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Dr. Stephen Kornfeld, retired oncologist from Bend, Oregon — avid birder who joined the MV Hondius cruise to spot rare birds. When the ship's physician fell ill, Kornfeld stepped in to monitor patients, comfort frightened passengers and assist crew. Identified as the 'mildly PCR positive' American passenger referenced in HHS May 10 statement. Kornfeld explained on CNN (May 12): two swabs sent to two Dutch labs — one negative, one 'faintly positive' — result described as 'intermediate.' CDC conducting further confirmatory testing. UPDATE May 14: CDC officially states there are ZERO confirmed U.S. hantavirus cases — Kornfeld's intermediate result does not yet meet CDC's case confirmation threshold. Had flu-like symptoms in early April on ship (3 days night sweats, chills, mild respiratory issues, fatigue for 2.5 weeks) but recovered. Now feeling 'great... 100%' and asymptomatic. On WhatsApp with family. All 17 Americans voluntarily committing to 42-day quarantine. Two exposures: (1) socialized with index case (P002/PEDB43) before outbreak identified; (2) provided medical care to symptomatic passengers. UPDATE May 15: Subsequent PCR tests came back NEGATIVE — moved from Nebraska biocontainment unit to the National Quarantine Unit to join the other 15 American passengers. CDC confirms 0 US cases. Yahoo News / CDC, May 15.
cases.reporter: People Magazine / Statesman Journal / CNN / HHS / CNBC / CDC press conference May 13-14

cases.name: American Passenger — Symptomatic, PCR NEGATIVE Confirmed, Emory SCDU (Atlanta)

location.venue: Emory University Hospital — Serious Communicable Diseases Unit (SCDU), Biocontainment Unit

transport.notes: US DoS airlift — TFS → Washington D.C. → Nebraska

cases.source_notes: ABC News live updates / HHS, May 11 2026
cases.clinical_notes: American MV Hondius passenger — symptomatic but PCR NEGATIVE confirmed by HHS May 12 via X post: 'The mildly symptomatic U.S. passenger taken to the @EmoryUniversity RESPTC in Atlanta tested negative for the Andes variant of hantavirus.' At Emory SCDU biocontainment unit. Transferred from UNMC Omaha to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta (~9am May 11) with partner PUSEMORY01 (asymptomatic). Fox5 initially reported positive — HHS officially confirmed negative. Still under observation at Emory given symptoms, even with negative PCR.

cases.name: American Passenger — Asymptomatic, Emory SCDU Monitoring (Atlanta)

location.venue: Emory University Hospital — Serious Communicable Diseases Unit (SCDU), monitoring

transport.notes: US DoS airlift TFS → Washington D.C. → Omaha; transferred Omaha → Atlanta May 11

cases.source_notes: ABC News live updates / Emory University Hospital statement, May 11 2026
cases.clinical_notes: American MV Hondius passenger — asymptomatic, transferred from Omaha to Emory University Hospital SCDU, Atlanta on May 11 2026. Emory confirmed: 'undergoing evaluation and monitoring.' This passenger and PUSRESPTC01 are a couple — both transferred together from UNMC Omaha to Emory via special flight to Hartsfield-Jackson (~9am May 11), transported by Grady ambulance. No symptoms reported as of May 11. Under observation due to close-contact exposure and precautionary monitoring.

cases.name: Kansas Contacts (3) — High-Risk Exposure, Not on Ship
cases.source_notes: Kansas KDHE statement / USA Today, May 12 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Three Kansas residents with 'high-risk exposure' to a confirmed Andes virus case — NOT aboard MV Hondius. Close contacts of an infected person who was symptomatic while traveling. Kansas KDHE (citing CDC): classified as high-risk because of close contact with a confirmed symptomatic case. All three asymptomatic as of May 12. 'No suspected cases of the virus in Kansas and the risk to the public remains extremely low.' Under monitoring.

cases.name: Maryland Resident(s) — Monitoring (Connection to MV Hondius)
cases.source_notes: Yahoo News / CDC, May 15 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Maryland is among the 10 US states confirmed by CDC as monitoring residents with connections to the MV Hondius outbreak. No further details disclosed. 10 states total: AZ, CA, GA, KS, MD, MN, NJ, TX, VA, WA. No symptoms reported. No confirmed cases.

cases.name: Washington State Contacts (6) — Flight Contacts + 1 Ship Passenger
cases.source_notes: Washington State DOH / USA Today, May 12 2026
cases.clinical_notes:
UPDATE May 17 2026: Washington state now investigating 6 potential Andes hantavirus cases — up from 3 as of May 12. Breakdown: (1) 5 flight contacts on an international flight with a Hondius passenger who was later diagnosed with hantavirus — 4 are King County residents, 1 from eastern Washington. (2) 1 MV Hondius ship passenger from Washington state — asymptomatic, at UNMC quarantine unit Omaha. All 6 being monitored for symptoms. Washington State DOH confirms all are being monitored. Dr. Sandra J. Valenciano (King County Health Officer): risk to King County residents remains low.

SEPARATE UNRELATED CASE: A sixth Washington resident tested positive for Sin Nombre hantavirus (Chelan-Douglas Health District, May 16) — this is a separate rodent-transmitted strain, unrelated to the Andes virus/MV Hondius outbreak. Sin Nombre is the most common US hantavirus and is NOT transmissible person-to-person.
cases.reporter: Washington State DOH / NBC News / USA Today

cases.name: Dutch Hospital Workers (12) — Preventive Quarantine, Sample Exposure

exposures.notes: Processing blood and urine samples from a confirmed hantavirus-infected patient without full precautions

cases.source_notes: NYT / Dutch hospital statement, May 13 2026
cases.clinical_notes: 12 staff at Radboudumc Hospital, Nijmegen quarantined for 6 weeks after handling blood and urine from a confirmed MV Hondius hantavirus patient admitted May 7 without following the strictest protocols. Dutch Health Minister Sophie Hermans told parliament: 'strict procedures were followed, but not the very strictest procedures that apply in cases involving this hantavirus.' Infection risk described as 'small' but hospital chose precautionary quarantine. Patient care at Radboudumc continued uninterrupted. Minister Hermans: 'It really is a different situation than with COVID. With the knowledge we have and the measures we are taking, we are confident we can keep this virus under control.'
cases.reporter: Yahoo News / Radboudumc / Dutch Health Ministry

cases.name: Minnesota Resident — Brief Overseas Exposure (Monitoring)
cases.source_notes: Minnesota Department of Health / Yahoo News, May 13 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Minnesota is the 10th US state monitoring residents for potential hantavirus exposure. One person 'who may have briefly been exposed overseas to someone who was on board the MV Hondius cruise and tested positive for hantavirus (Andes virus).' Per MDH: person is cooperative, monitored daily, no symptoms. Not aboard the ship — secondary contact exposure overseas. States monitoring as of May 13: AZ, CA, GA, KS, MD, MN, NJ, TX, VA, WA (10 states).
cases.reporter: Minnesota Department of Health / Yahoo News

cases.name: Italy Cluster (4) — 1 SYMPTOMATIC UNDER ANALYSIS at Lazzaro Spallanzani Institute, Rome

location.venue: Lazzaro Spallanzani National Institute for Infectious Diseases, Rome

cases.source_notes: TeleSUR / Italian Health Ministry, May 13 2026
cases.clinical_notes: UPDATE May 13 2026: ONE ITALIAN NOW SYMPTOMATIC — samples sent to Lazzaro Spallanzani National Institute for Infectious Diseases, Rome for urgent analysis. The symptomatic case is a young man from the Calabria region who was in voluntary quarantine after brief contact with a Dutch woman (P002/PEDB43, later deceased) on a KLM flight. He is one of four Italians under monitoring who were on that KLM flight. Italy's Health Ministry has introduced a 'maximum precaution' protocol: 42-day quarantines and daily monitoring for high-risk contacts. Ministry: risk to general public is 'very low.' PRIOR STATUS: The other three (Argentine tourist with pneumonia, British tourist in Milan, British tourist's companion) had previously tested negative. The Calabria man's symptomatic status requires Spallanzani analysis to rule in or out. KLM flight contact — not a ship passenger.
cases.reporter: TeleSUR / Lazzaro Spallanzani Institute / Italian Health Ministry

cases.name: Jake Rosmarin — Boston Travel Influencer, UNMC Quarantine (Asymptomatic)

transport.notes: US DoS airlift TFS → Washington D.C. → Omaha

cases.source_notes: USA Today, May 13 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Jake Rosmarin — Boston-based travel influencer and one of 18 Americans aboard MV Hondius. Currently asymptomatic in UNMC National Quarantine Unit, Omaha. Voluntarily committing to full 42-day quarantine. Has fiancé Alex in Boston. 'From the beginning, I made that decision immediately, that I want to be here for that 42 days, because I know that if I'm here, I'm going to be in the best care possible.' Room has stationary bike, thermometer; bloodwork done daily. Has been speaking with media frequently. Quote on public risk: 'It's not COVID. It's scary, or scarier, for people like us, who are at risk with exposure after being on a ship with people for five-to-six weeks. But, for people who are never coming in contact with us... I don't think it's anything anyone needs to worry about.' One of 16 Americans now in the UNMC quarantine unit (Dr. Kornfeld moved from biocontainment to quarantine unit May 15 after subsequent tests came back negative).

cases.name: Angela Perryman — US Citizen (Ecuador), UNMC Omaha — Mandatory Federal Quarantine Order

transport.notes: US DoS airlift — TFS → Washington D.C. → Nebraska (UNMC)

cases.source_notes: New York Times, May 18 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Angela Perryman, 47, US citizen currently living in Ecuador, home in South Florida. MV Hondius passenger — had passing contact with one passenger who died from hantavirus. Tested NEGATIVE. Asymptomatic. Was planning to leave UNMC to isolate in Florida when she was handed the mandatory order. Officials had publicly stated the stay was voluntary; Trump admin took pains to avoid even using the word 'quarantine' given post-COVID political sensitivity. On May 19, received a federal mandatory quarantine order requiring her to remain at UNMC through May 31. Authorized by CDC acting director Jay Bhattacharya. Only the second mandatory federal quarantine order in about a half century; first since COVID pandemic. Perryman: 'I am angry. I feel betrayed. I'm being imprisoned. It's a nice prison. But this is a prison. Let's be clear: I am being detained against my will.' And: 'If they can do this to me they could do it to anybody.' UPDATE May 21: Planning to legally challenge her confinement. Legal experts divided — Lawrence Gostin (Georgetown): 'The evidence in favor of CDC is very strong.' James Hodge (ASU): 'There's a very real legal case that could be made to say: This is a due process violation. You're infringing my liberty without direct sufficient evidence.' CDC has only publicly acknowledged two mandatory orders and has not named those involved. — NPR, May 21 2026

cases.name: Illinois Resident — Possible Hantavirus, NOT Tied to Cruise Ship
cases.source_notes: USA Today, May 13 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Possible hantavirus case in Illinois explicitly confirmed as NOT tied to the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak. Source of exposure under investigation — likely rodent/environmental contact, which is the typical transmission route for hantavirus in the US. Details limited as of May 13. Important distinction: this would be a separate, independent hantavirus exposure unrelated to the Andes strain ship cluster. Generation listed as 0 (independent index case if confirmed). Status and details pending further reporting.

cases.name: Spanish Passenger — Gómez Ulla Confirmed Positive

location.venue: Gómez Ulla Central Defence Hospital — Floor 22, Maximum Isolation / UATAN Unit

cases.source_notes: Al Jazeera / Euronews / The Spanish Eye, May 11-12 2026
cases.clinical_notes: One of 14 Spanish MV Hondius passengers quarantined at Gómez Ulla Military Hospital, Madrid. Initial PCR on arrival (May 10-11) came back positive provisionally; confirmed positive by second PCR on May 12. Was initially asymptomatic upon arrival but developed low-grade fever and mild respiratory symptoms. Currently stable on floor 22 with maximum isolation — no evident clinical deterioration. Health Minister Mónica García announced result via social media. If confirmed, will transfer to UATAN high-level isolation unit. Part of PESP14 group — the only positive among 14 (other 13 tested negative on first PCR). Spain set May 10 as 'day zero' for the 42-day quarantine. This is the 10th confirmed case in the MV Hondius outbreak per WHO count.
cases.reporter: Al Jazeera / Euronews / Spain Health Ministry / The Spanish Eye

cases.name: UK Arrowe Park Evacuees (22) — 20 British + 1 German + 1 Japanese

location.venue: Arrowe Park Hospital, Wirral — Isolation Wing → Home Self-Isolation

transport.notes: Dedicated repatriation flight Tenerife → Manchester Airport, May 10. Transferred by private coach to Arrowe Park Hospital.

cases.source_notes: The Tab / ITV News Granada / UKHSA, May 10-13 2026
cases.clinical_notes: 22 MV Hondius evacuees (20 British nationals + 1 German UK-resident + 1 Japanese passenger) arrived Manchester airport May 10 on a dedicated flight and were transferred by private coach to Arrowe Park Hospital on the Wirral for 72 hours of precautionary isolation. All healthy and asymptomatic on arrival per Prof Robin May (UKHSA). Strict infection control: PPE including face masks for passengers, crew, drivers, and medical teams throughout. As of May 13: being discharged to 42 days of home self-isolation in self-contained flats with phone access. Public transport prohibited during isolation. Specialist support team on-site. 6 individuals already released to home/suitable accommodation after negative PCR tests. If symptoms develop → transfer to Royal Liverpool University Hospital Tropical & Infectious Diseases Unit. Additionally, 10 more passengers/crew from St Helena and Ascension Island expected to arrive for similar monitoring. Note: excludes previously evacuated British nationals P003 (in SA), P872E8 (in Netherlands), and PTRISTAN01/PTRDC01 (Tristan da Cunha).
cases.reporter: UKHSA / The Tab / ITV News Granada

cases.name: Belgian Passengers (2) — Antwerp University Hospital

location.venue: Antwerp University Hospital (UZA) → Home monitoring by Flemish health authorities

cases.source_notes: Euronews, May 11 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Two Belgian MV Hondius passengers received at Antwerp University Hospital (UZA) on Sunday May 11 for medical assessment. Consistent with ship manifest showing 2 Belgian guests. If asymptomatic (which they are), to be sent home and monitored through follow-up by Flemish health authorities. Belgium is among six European countries receiving repatriated MV Hondius passengers.

cases.name: US Flight Contacts — April 25 Johannesburg Flight (16 people)

location.venue: Monitored by CDC — locations not disclosed

transport.notes: Airlink flight 4Z 301 — Saint Helena → Johannesburg, April 25 2026

cases.source_notes: Firstpost / CDC / Dr. David Fitter, May 15 2026
cases.clinical_notes: 16 Americans who were passengers on the April 25 Airlink flight 4Z 301 (Saint Helena → Johannesburg), which carried index case PEDB43 (the Dutch woman who died in Johannesburg on April 26). These are Gen 2 contacts — NOT MV Hondius passengers. Announced May 15 by CDC/Dr. David Fitter. CDC is 'monitoring all Americans that potentially would have been exposed, whether in the U.S. or abroad.' CDC declined to disclose where in the US these individuals are located. All in quarantine/monitoring. No confirmed cases among them as of May 15. Brings total US monitoring to 41: 18 ship passengers (UNMC/Emory biocontainment) + 7 pre-detection St. Helena returnees (state-monitored) + 16 Johannesburg flight contacts (this group) = 41.

cases.name: Portuguese National — Suspected Case, TESTED NEGATIVE (Retesting Required)

transport.notes: Traveled by plane as part of the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship with confirmed cases

cases.source_notes: Euronews / RTP / DGS Portugal, May 20 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Adult Portuguese national admitted to São Francisco Xavier Hospital, Lisbon on May 19 with flu-like symptoms. Traveled by plane as part of the hantavirus outbreak linked to MV Hondius. First suspected hantavirus case in Portugal. Initial test came back NEGATIVE. Per DGS (Directorate-General of Health) protocol, even a negative result within the 42-day maximum incubation period requires retesting and reclassification. Patient transferred to Curry Cabral reference hospital (also served as COVID-19 reference centre; received Lisbon's first COVID patient). DGS defines a suspected case as any person who shared transport with a confirmed/probable case, or who had contact with an MV Hondius passenger/crew member since April 5, 2026 and presents symptoms. Not yet confirmed — monitoring continues.

cases.name: MV Hondius Crew Member — Confirmed Positive (Andes Virus), Netherlands

transport.notes: Remained aboard MV Hondius for return voyage to Rotterdam (arrived May 18)

cases.source_notes: RIVM / Reuters / GMA Network, May 22 2026
cases.clinical_notes: MV Hondius crew member confirmed positive for Andes hantavirus while in quarantine in the Netherlands. First confirmed crew member case — all previous confirmed cases have been passengers. Crew member remained aboard the ship for the return voyage to Rotterdam (ship arrived May 18), then entered quarantine in the Netherlands. Tested positive during quarantine and admitted to hospital as a precaution. In isolation. RIVM: 'The Andes virus has been detected in one person who was in quarantine in the Netherlands. The patient has since been admitted to the hospital as a precaution and is in isolation.' Andes virus is the human-to-human transmissible strain. Nationality not disclosed. Identity not disclosed. — Reuters/GMA, May 22 2026

cases.name: Dutch Crew Member — Confirmed Positive, Disembarked Spain (MV Hondius)
cases.source_notes: CIDRAP, May 27 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Dutch crew member who disembarked the MV Hondius in Spain (Tenerife, May 10) and was being monitored for hantavirus. Confirmed positive in late May — one of the two new cases bringing the outbreak total to 13. Separate from PNLCREW01 (Dutch crew member who remained aboard for return voyage to Rotterdam and tested positive in Netherlands). Both are Dutch nationals but disembarked at different points. Being monitored in Spain at time of confirmation. — CIDRAP, May 27 2026

cases.name: Spanish Citizen — Confirmed Positive, MV Hondius Passenger, Hospital Madrid
cases.source_notes: CIDRAP / Spain Health Ministry, May 25-27 2026
cases.clinical_notes: Spanish citizen who was aboard the MV Hondius, isolated at a hospital in Madrid. Spain confirmed positive May 25, 2026. Second confirmed Spanish case from MV Hondius (PESP_GU01 was the first, confirmed earlier at Gómez Ulla Defense Hospital). One of two new cases bringing outbreak total to 13. — CIDRAP, May 27 2026

edges.notes: Married couple sharing Cabin 406 for entire voyage. Onset gap of 48 h consistent with close-quarters person-to-person transmission.
British crew member provided care to index case without adequate PPE over 3-4 days. 9-day onset lag consistent with hantavirus incubation.

edges.event: Crew Common Areas / Shared Ventilation — Deck 2
edges.notes: Dutch crew member exposure likely indirect via shared spaces and ventilation with P003. Serology pending.
